content:
# Matthew Chapter 21 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_021_page_001.jpg, MAT_021_page_002.jpg

1. And when they drew near to Hierosolyma and came to Bethphage, to the Mount of the Olives, then the Yeshua sent two disciples,

2. saying to them, "Go into the village opposite you, and immediately you will find a donkey having been tied and a colt with her. Having untied, bring [them] to me.

3. And if anyone says anything to you, you will say, 'The Lord has need of them,' and immediately he will send them."

4. This happened in order that what was spoken through the prophet might be fulfilled, saying,

5. "Say to the daughter of Sion, 'Behold, the king of you comes to you, gentle and mounted upon a donkey and upon a colt, [the] son of a beast-of-burden.'"

6. And having gone, the disciples and having done as the Yeshua commanded them,

7. they brought the donkey and the colt and placed upon them the garments, and he sat upon them.

8. And the most [of the] crowd spread the garments of themselves on the road, and others were cutting branches from the trees and spreading [them] on the road.

9. And the crowds, the [ones] going before him and the [ones] following, were crying out, saying, "Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessed [is] the [one] coming in [the] name of [the] Lord! Hosanna in the highest!"

10. And of him having entered into Hierosolyma, all the city was shaken, saying, "Who is this?"

11. And the crowds were saying, "This is the prophet, the Yeshua, the [one] from Nazareth of the Galilaia."

12. And the Yeshua entered into the temple and cast out all the [ones] selling and buying in the temple, and the tables of the money-changers he overturned and the seats of the [ones] selling the doves.

13. And he says to them, "It has been written, 'The house of me a house of prayer will be called,' but you are making it a cave of robbers."

14. And blind [ones] and lame [ones] came to him in the temple, and he healed them.

15. But having seen, the chief priests and the scribes, the wonderful [things] which he did, and the children crying out in the temple and saying, "Hosanna to the Son of David," they were indignant

16. and said to him, "Do you hear what these are saying?" And the Yeshua says to them, "Yes. Have you never read, 'Out of [the] mouth of infants and nursing [ones] you prepared praise'?"

17. And having left them, he went out of the city to Bethania and lodged there.

18. And early [in the] morning, returning to the city, he hungered.

19. And having seen one fig tree upon the road, he came to it and found nothing on it except leaves only. And he says to it, "No longer from you may fruit come into the age." And immediately the fig tree withered.

20. And having seen, the disciples marveled, saying, "How did the fig tree immediately wither?"

21. And answering, the Yeshua said to them, "Truly I say to you, if you have faith and do not doubt, not only the [thing] of the fig tree will you do, but even if you say to this mountain, 'Be taken up and cast into the sea,' it will happen.

22. And all things, as many as you ask in the prayer believing, you will receive."

23. And of him having come into the temple, the chief priests and the elders of the people came to him [while he was] teaching, saying, "By what authority do you do these things, and who gave to you this authority?"

24. And answering, the Yeshua said to them, "I also will ask you one word, which if you tell me, I also will tell you by what authority I do these things.

25. The baptism of Ioannes, from where was it? From heaven or from men?" And they were reasoning among themselves, saying, "If we say, 'From heaven,' he will say to us, 'Why then did you not believe him?'

26. But if we say, 'From men,' we fear the crowd; for all hold Ioannes as a prophet."

27. And answering the Yeshua, they said, "We do not know." He also said to them, "Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things.

28. But what do you think? A man had two children. Having come to the first, he said, 'Child, go today, work in the vineyard.'

29. And answering, he said, 'I do not wish.' But afterward, having regretted, he went.

30. And having come to the other, he said likewise. And answering, he said, 'I [go], lord,' and did not go.

31. Which of the two did the will of the father?" They say, "The first." The Yeshua says to them, "Truly I say to you that the tax-collectors and the prostitutes go before you into the kingdom of the God.

32. For Ioannes came to you in [the] way of righteousness, and you did not believe him; but the tax-collectors and the prostitutes believed him. And you, having seen, did not even regret afterward to believe him.

33. Another parable hear: A man was, a householder, who planted a vineyard and placed around it a fence, and dug in it a winepress, and built a tower, and leased it to farmers, and went-abroad.

34. And when the season of the fruits drew near, he sent the slaves of him to the farmers to receive the fruits of him.

35. And having taken, the farmers the slaves of him, one they beat, and one they killed, and one they stoned.

36. Again he sent other slaves, more than the first, and they did to them likewise.

37. And afterward he sent to them the son of him, saying, 'They will respect the son of me.'

38. But the farmers, having seen the son, said among themselves, 'This is the heir. Come, let us kill him and possess the inheritance of him.'

39. And having taken him, they cast [him] out of the vineyard and killed [him].

40. When therefore the lord of the vineyard comes, what will he do to the farmers those?"

41. They say to him, "The evil [ones] he will evilly destroy, and the vineyard he will lease to other farmers, who will give to him the fruits in the seasons of them."

42. The Yeshua says to them, "Have you never read in the Scriptures, 'A stone which the builders rejected, this became the head of [the] corner. From [the] Lord this happened, and it is wonderful in the eyes of us'?

43. Because of this I say to you that the kingdom of the God will be taken from you and will be given to a nation producing the fruits of it.

44. And the [one] having fallen upon this stone will be shattered; and upon whomever it falls, it will crush him."

45. And having heard, the chief priests and the Pharisaioi the parables of him knew that concerning them he speaks.

46. And seeking to seize him, they feared the crowds, since as a prophet they held him.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **ἐσείσθη (v.10):** "Was shaken" — from σείω (to shake, as in earthquake/seismic). The whole city is shaken — the same verb used for the earthquake at the crucifixion (27:51) and resurrection (28:2). Jesus' entry creates a tremor.

2. **Zechariah 9:9 (v.5):** Matthew's quotation conflates Isaiah 62:11 ("Say to the daughter of Zion") with Zechariah 9:9 ("your king comes... gentle, riding on a donkey"). Matthew includes both the donkey and the colt, following the Hebrew parallelism literally.

3. **Isaiah 56:7 and Jeremiah 7:11 (v.13):** Jesus combines "house of prayer" (Isaiah 56:7) with "cave of robbers" (Jeremiah 7:11). The Jeremiah echo is especially ominous — in context, Jeremiah warns that the temple will be destroyed for such corruption.

4. **v.43 — "a nation producing fruits":** This is the interpretive key to the wicked tenants parable. The kingdom is transferred from those who fail to produce fruit to those who will — a warning to Israel's leadership, not to Israel as a whole.

— EMET, AI Translator
